page: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv6 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. The server features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2.0 threads per core. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8). These hardware capabilities translate to strong benchmark results. In Geekbench testing, the server achieved a single-core score of 2122.0 and a multi-core score of 20088.0, with notable performance in image processing and software compilation. Passmark CPU testing yielded a score of 62325.35, with strong results in extended instructions and encryption.

With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D48ls_v6 is optimized for compute-heavy workloads rather than memory-intensive applications. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show strong performance, reaching 249.73 GB/sec for large block read-only operations. The server demonstrates high efficiency in text and data processing, as evidenced by a Redis SET performance of 64.82 million operations per second and a static web serving throughput of 78.25 GB/sec. Additionally, the processor's AMX and AVX-512 extensions support efficient low-latency LLM inference, processing 243.97 tokens per second for medium 7B models. This server is suited for compute-bound tasks, including web serving, software compilation, cryptography, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48ls_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz, delivering 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores, paired with 96.0 GB of RAM. This low-memory configuration lacks local storage but supports premium storage options and lacks a GPU. The hardware is highly optimized for compute-heavy tasks, utilizing advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. Benchmarks indicate strong performance in software compilation, cryptography, and database operations, alongside high memory bandwidth. The D48ls_v6 is well-suited for compute-intensive workloads such as static web serving, Redis databases, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for high-throughput compute workloads and software compilation.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs and 96 GB of RAM, optimized for compute-intensive workloads and web serving.
tagline: A dedicated forty-eight vCPU virtual machine with a low-memory profile, optimized for high-throughput compute and matrix-accelerated workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions for accelerated matrix operations
Delivers strong performance in software compilation, cryptography, and database operations
Lacks local storage but supports premium external storage options
Suited for static web serving, Redis deployments, and medium-scale LLM inference
